title: Graph Valid Tree
problem_statement: Check whether the undirected graph with n nodes and given edges forms a valid tree.

examples.explanation: The graph is connected and acyclic, so it is a valid tree.

intuition: A valid tree has exactly n-1 edges and is connected; use union-find or BFS to verify both conditions.

approaches.name: Union-Find cycle detection
approaches.description: Union endpoints of each edge and check for cycles or missing connectivity by comparing component count.
approaches.why_not: Counting edges alone is insufficient without verifying connectivity.

interview_qa.q: Why must a tree have n-1 edges?
interview_qa.a: Connected n nodes require at least n-1 edges, and any extra edge creates a cycle.

interview_qa.q: How can a graph fail to be a tree despite having n-1 edges?
interview_qa.a: It may be disconnected, so checking both edges count and connectivity is necessary.
